title: coprosma calycina
intro: Coprosma calycina, also known as the New Zealand Karamu or Coprosma, is a shrub native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, gastrointestinal problems, and fever, due to its health benefits such as antibacterial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the Coprosma calycina are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
